key_insight: A hypernetwork-conditioned hybrid physics-data surrogate can learn microstructure-dependent mechanics from small datasets, making multiscale optimization of graded mycelium composites tractable.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster design of sustainable, functionally graded materials by linking manufacturable microstructure choices to macroscale stress performance.
method: Train a hybrid surrogate for microscale mechanical response.
Condition surrogate weights on microstructural variables via a hypernetwork.
Extend conditioning to manufacturing variables for direct process-to-performance design.
evidence_signal: The conditioned surrogate was validated against full FE² simulation and used to optimize a graded disk, reducing peak stress by 42% versus a random-microstructure design.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of the limited training set, so extrapolation to unseen microstructures or manufacturing conditions should be treated carefully.
paper_title: Microstructure-Conditioned Surrogate Models for Graded Multiscale Optimization of Mycelium Composites
